geometry_verification.reason: No geometry generated due to infeasibility

artifact_verification.reason: No artifacts generated due to infeasibility

analytical_evidence.note: Required LC product is ~100-1000x larger than any practical on-chip spiral+MIM combination. Reaching 5 MHz needs L ~= 10.13 uH (with 100 pF) or C ~= 101 nF (with 10 nH), neither realizable on-chip.

simulation_evidence.note: No simulation possible for an infeasible target

physics_verification.note: Target frequency is not achievable with practical on-chip LC components

warnings: 5 MHz LC resonator is INFEASIBLE for a practical fully on-chip passive design
Required LC product (1.013e-15 s^2) exceeds practical on-chip limits by ~100-1000x
L = 10 nH, C = 100 pF resonates at 159 MHz (31x higher than target), not near 5 MHz
Parasitics dominate and on-chip spiral Q (~2-10) is too low at 5 MHz
recommendation: Use discrete components, a crystal/ceramic resonator, or an active (gyrator) LC realization for 5 MHz. Minimum comfortable on-chip resonance with these passives is ~159 MHz.
